```mermaid
mindmap
  root(From the Diary of Anne Frank)
    1. Anne Frank: The Author
      1.1 German-born Jewish girl
      1.2 Born: 12 June 1929
      1.3 Died: Feb/March 1945 (Bergen-Belsen)
      1.4 A renowned Holocaust victim
    2. Historical Context: World War II
      2.1 German occupation of the Netherlands
      2.2 Nazi persecution of Jewish population
      2.3 Family moved to Amsterdam (escaping Nazis in Germany)
      2.4 Trapped when Nazi occupation extended
    3. Life in Hiding (The Annex)
      3.1 Location: Hidden rooms in Otto Frank’s office building, Amsterdam
      3.2 Duration: From July 1942 for two years
      3.3 Companions: Family and four friends
      3.4 Tragic End:
        3.4.1 Group betrayed
        3.4.2 Transported to concentration camp system
        3.4.3 Anne died of typhus (within days of sister, Margot Frank)
    4. The Diary Itself
      4.1 Origin: Given to Anne for her thirteenth birthday (12 June 1942)
      4.2 Content: Chronicles events from 12 June 1942 to 1 August 1944
      4.3 Original Language: Dutch
      4.4 Publication:
        4.4.1 Saved after the war
        4.4.2 Published by her father, Otto Frank (only survivor)
        4.4.3 English Title: "The Diary of a Young Girl"
      4.5 Impact and Significance:
        4.5.1 Translated into many languages
        4.5.2 One of the world’s most widely read books
        4.5.3 Basis for films, TV, theatrical productions, opera
        4.5.4 Described as "work of a mature and insightful mind"
        4.5.5 Provides "intimate examination of daily life under Nazi occupation"
    5. Anne's Motivation for Writing
      5.1 Initial Hesitation:
        5.1.1 Never written anything before
        5.1.2 Doubts interest in a thirteen-year-old schoolgirl's "musings"
      5.2 Overriding Need:
        5.2.1 "I feel like writing"
        5.2.2 "Greater need to get all kinds of things off my chest"
    6. "Paper has more patience than people." (Anne's Realization)
      6.1 Context: Feeling depressed, bored, listless at home
      6.2 Meaning: Paper listens without judgment or weariness
      6.3 Purpose of Diary: A private confidant
      6.4 Condition for Sharing: "Unless I should ever find a real friend"
    7. The Search for a "True Friend"
      7.1 Core Problem: "I don't have a friend."
      7.2 Apparent Social Life:
        7.2.1 Loving parents and a sixteen-year-old sister
        7.2.2 About thirty people she can call friends
        7.2.3 Family, loving aunts, a good home
        7.2.4 On the surface: "seem to have everything"
      7.3 Lack of Deep Connection:
        7.3.1 With friends: only "having a good time"
        7.3.2 Cannot talk about "anything but ordinary everyday things"
        7.3.3 Inability to confide deeply or truly connect
    8. Types of Personal Records (Vocabulary from Activity)
      8.1 Diary: Daily thoughts/feelings/events
      8.2 Journal: Full record of journey/period/event
      8.3 Log: Official record with times/dates
      8.4 Memoir(s): Record of own life/experiences (usually famous person)
```
